title: Correlations
text: reprho(), first need create replicate weights. Using included repdata data, using "LANA" method: make easier specify arguments, advised create also "repsetup" object. create three setups example: one without groups, one groups without exclusions, one groups exclusions (excluding group 2):
code: RW <- repcreate(df = repdata,                 wt = "wt",                 jkzone = "jkzones",                 jkrep = "jkrep",                 method = "LANA") # No groups STNG <- repsetup(repwt = RW, wt = "wt", df = repdata, method = "LANA")  # With groups STGR <- repsetup(repwt = RW, wt = "wt", df = repdata, method = "LANA",                  group = "GROUP")  # With groups and exclusions STGE <- repsetup(repwt = RW, wt = "wt", df = repdata, method = "LANA",                  group = "GROUP", exclude = "GR2")

title: Correlations

title: Replicate weights
text: importantly, need decide method. methods available ILSAs, able use official one experiment . creating replicate weights, 4 methods implemented, can use method name ILSA names: "JK-full", corresponds "TIMSS", "PIRLS" "LANA". "JK-half", corresponds "ICILS", "ICCS", "CIVED". "JK2-half-1PV", corresponds "oldTIMSS", "oldPIRLS", "RLII" (TIMSS PIRLS conducted 2015). "FAY-0.5", corresponds "PISA" "TALIS". data corresponds TIMSS 1999, advised use either old method TIMSS replicate results ("oldTIMSS"), new method TIMSS compare new results ("TIMSS").

title: Survey Weighted Mixed-Effects Models with Plausible Values — WeMixPV
text: Fits linear mixed-effects model using mix plausible values.

title: Survey Weighted Mixed-Effects Models with Plausible Values — WeMixPV
code: WeMixPV(formula, data = NULL, weights = NULL, pvs, relatedpvs = TRUE, ...)

title: Survey Weighted Mixed-Effects Models with Plausible Values — WeMixPV
text: formula formula object style lme4 creates model. data data frame containing raw data model. weights character vector names weight variables found data frame starts units (level 1) increasing (larger groups). pvs list indicating variables formula replaced plausible values variables. details check examples. relatedpvs logical value indicating pvs drawn model, number plausible values. TRUE (default), total \(n\) estimations done, \(n\) number plausible values plausible value variable. FALSE, total \(n_1 \times n_2 \times n_...\) estimations done, \(n_i\) number plausible values plausible value variable. ... Arguments passed WeMix::mix cWeights logical, set TRUE use conditional weights. Otherwise, mix expects unconditional weights. center_group list name element name aggregation level, element formula variable names group mean centered; example group mean center gender age within group student:  list("student"= ~gender+age), default value NULL perform group mean centering. center_grand formula variable names  grand mean centered, example center variable education overall mean education: ~education. Default NULL centering. max_iteration optional integer, non-linear models fit adaptive quadrature limits number iterations allowed quitting. Defaults  10. used likelihood surface flat, models may run  long time without converging. nQuad optional integer  number quadrature points evaluate models solved adaptive quadrature. non-linear models evaluated adaptive quadrature. See notes additional guidelines. run logical; TRUE runs model FALSE provides partial output debugging testing. applies non-linear models evaluated adaptive quadrature. verbose logical, default FALSE; set TRUE print results intermediate steps adaptive quadrature. applies non-linear models. acc0 deprecated; ignored. keepAdapting logical, set TRUE adaptive quadrature adapt every Newton step. Defaults FALSE. FALSE used faster (less accurate) results. applies non-linear models. start optional numeric vector representing point model start optimization; takes shape c(coef, vars) results (see help). fast logical; deprecated family family; optionally used specify generalized linear mixed models. Currently binomial() poisson() supported.

title: Survey Weighted Mixed-Effects Models with Plausible Values — WeMixPV
code: # Prepare data weights repdata2 <- repdata repdata2$wt1 <- repdata2$wt # weight level 1 repdata2$wt2 <- 1 # weight level 2   # Null model - with PVs ## Named list, with element names matching formula variables pvs = list(MATH = paste0("Math",1:5))   m1 <- WeMixPV(formula = MATH ~ 1 + (1|GROUP), # Intercept varies across GROUP              pvs = pvs, # Named list              data = repdata2, # Data frame              weights = c("wt1","wt2")) # Weights vector m1 #> 5 models were estimated. #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------  #> Multilevel results with PVs: #>   #> Call:  #> WeMixPV(formula = MATH ~ 1 + (1 | GROUP), data = repdata2, weights = c("wt1",  #>     "wt2"), pvs = pvs) #>  #> Random effects: #>                    Estimate Std. Error   t value          df     Pr(>|t|) #> GROUP.(Intercept) 0.2332214 0.11973218  1.947859 2282.198170 5.155428e-02 #> Residual          0.8132685 0.02299559 35.366280    7.250355 2.186994e-09 #>  #> Fixed effects: #>                Estimate Std. Error   t value        df  Pr(>|t|) #> (Intercept) 0.004824544  0.3417468 0.0141173 181384501 0.9887364 #> -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- #> Estimated models: #> Math1 ~ 1 + (1 | GROUP) #> Math2 ~ 1 + (1 | GROUP) #> Math3 ~ 1 + (1 | GROUP) #> Math4 ~ 1 + (1 | GROUP) #> Math5 ~ 1 + (1 | GROUP)  ## Fixed effects m1$fixef #>                Estimate Std. Error   t value        df  Pr(>|t|) #> (Intercept) 0.004824544  0.3417468 0.0141173 181384501 0.9887364  ## Random effects m1$ranef #>                    Estimate Std. Error   t value          df     Pr(>|t|) #> GROUP.(Intercept) 0.2332214 0.11973218  1.947859 2282.198170 5.155428e-02 #> Residual          0.8132685 0.02299559 35.366280    7.250355 2.186994e-09  ## Models for each PV summary(m1$models) #>       Length Class        Mode #> Math1 22     WeMixResults list #> Math2 22     WeMixResults list #> Math3 22     WeMixResults list #> Math4 22     WeMixResults list #> Math5 22     WeMixResults list  # Multiple regression ## Named list, with element names matching formula variables pvs = list(MATH = paste0("Math",1:5))   m2 <- WeMixPV(formula = MATH ~ 1 + GENDER + SES + schoolSES + (1|GROUP),              pvs = pvs, # Named list              data = repdata2, # Data frame              weights = c("wt1","wt2")) # Weights vector m2 #> 5 models were estimated. #> --------------------------------------------------------------------------------  #> Multilevel results with PVs: #>   #> Call:  #> WeMixPV(formula = MATH ~ 1 + GENDER + SES + schoolSES + (1 |  #>     GROUP), data = repdata2, weights = c("wt1", "wt2"), pvs = pvs) #>  #> Random effects: #>                       Estimate   Std. Error    t value         df     Pr(>|t|) #> GROUP.(Intercept) 1.666843e-05 8.968191e-05  0.1858616 413.555578 8.526444e-01 #> Residual          5.591163e-01 3.337543e-02 16.7523312   5.787741 3.995963e-06 #>  #> Fixed effects: #>                Estimate Std. title: Linear Models with Replicate Weights — replm
text: formula object class "formula" (one can coerced class): symbolic description model fitted. pvs plausible values used, NULL. Otherwise list indicating variables formula replaced plausible values variables. details check examples. relatedpvs logical value indicating pvs drawn model. TRUE (default), total \(n\) estimations done, \(n\) number plausible values plausible value variable. FALSE, total \(n_1 \times n_2 \times n_...\) estimations done, \(n_i\) number plausible values plausible value variable. quiet logical value indicating progress status shown estimating models group. Default FALSE. summarize logical value indicating lm objects converted summary.lm summary.glm objects stripped certain elements reduce size output object. Default TRUE. setup optional list produced repsetup. df data frame. wt string specifying name column (within df) total weights. repwt string indicating common names replicate weights columns (within df), data frame replicate weights. group string specifying variable name (within df) used grouping. Categories group treated independent, e.g., countries. exclude vector indicating groups (format group) excluded pooled composite estimates. na.action function indicates happen     data contain NAs.  default set     na.action setting options,     na.fail unset.  ‘factory-fresh’     default na.omit.  Another possible value     NULL, action.  Value na.exclude can useful. method string indicating name replication method. Available options : "JK2-full", "JK2-half", "FAY-0.5", "JK2-half-1PV".  Additionally, ILSA names can used, defaulting : "TIMSS",  "PIRLS", "LANA" "JK2-full"; "ICILS", "ICCS", "CIVED" "JK2-half"; "PISA" "TALIS" "FAY-0.5"; "oldTIMSS", "oldPIRLS", "RLII" "JK2-half-1PV". Note "oldTIMSS" "oldPIRLS" refer method used TIMSS PIRLS 2015, within imputation variance estimated using 1 plausible value. aggregates string vector indicating aggregates included, options "pooled" "composite", options can used time. text: er vector list containing statistic interest (e.g., percent, mean, variance, regression coefficient). vector list length==1, function estimates standard errors without plausible values. list length>1, estimates standard errors plausible values. e0 numeric vector vector containing statistic interest (e.g., percent, mean, variance, regression coefficient), computed using total weights. scenarios without plausible values, e0 single value. scenarios plausible values, e0 vector length er. setup optional list produced repsetup. method string indicating name replication method. Available options : "JK2-full", "JK2-half", "FAY-0.5", "JK2-half-1PV".  Additionally, ILSA names can used, defaulting : "TIMSS",  "PIRLS", "LANA" "JK2-full"; "ICILS", "ICCS", "CIVED" "JK2-half"; "PISA" "TALIS" "FAY-0.5"; "oldTIMSS", "oldPIRLS", "RLII" "JK2-half-1PV". Note "oldTIMSS" "oldPIRLS" refer method used TIMSS PIRLS 2015, within imputation variance estimated using 1 plausible value. se numeric vector standard errors, used repsecomp() estimate composite standard error. title: Standard Error for Estimates with Replicate Weights and Plausible Values — repse
text: standard error.

title: Standard Error for Estimates with Replicate Weights and Plausible Values — repse
text: standard errors calculated using modifier \(m\), JK2-full: \(m = 0.5\); JK2-half: \(m = 1\); FAY-0.5: \(\frac{1}{R(1-0.5)^2}\). Depending statistic, one following formulas used. standard error involving plausible values calculated : $$\sqrt{m\times \sum_{r=1}^{R}(\varepsilon_r-\varepsilon_0)^2}.$$ standard error involving plausibles values replicate weights calculated : $$\sqrt{\left[ \sum_{p=1}^{P} \left( m\times \sum_{r=1}^{R}(\varepsilon_{rp}-\varepsilon_{0p})^2 \right)  \dfrac{1}{P}\right]+  \left[ \left(1+ \dfrac{1}{P} \right) \dfrac{\sum_{p=1}^{P} (\varepsilon_{0p}-\overline{\varepsilon}_{0p})^{2}}{P-1} \right]}.$$ standard error involving plausibles values without replicate weights calculated : $$\sqrt{  \dfrac{\sum_{p=1}^{P} SE^2_{\varepsilon_{0P}}}{P}+  \left[ \left(1+ \dfrac{1}{P} \right) \dfrac{\sum_{p=1}^{P} (\varepsilon_{0p}-\overline{\varepsilon}_{0p})^{2}}{P-1} \right]}.$$ standard error difference two statistics (\(\) \(b\)) independent samples calculated : $$\sqrt{SE_a^{2}+SE_b^{2}}.$$ standard error difference two statistics (\(\) \(b\)) dependent samples involving plausible values calculated : $$\sqrt{m\times \sum_{r=1}^R((a_r-b_r)-(a_0-b_0))^2}.$$ standard error difference two statistics (\(\) \(b\)) dependent samples involving plausible values calculated : $$\sqrt{\left[ \sum_{p=1}^{P} \left( m\times \sum_{r=1}^{R}((a_{rp}-b_{rp})-(a_{0p}-b_{0p}))^2 \right)  \dfrac{1}{P}\right]+  \left[ \left(1+ \dfrac{1}{P} \right) \dfrac{\sum_{p=1}^{P} \left((a_{0p}-b_{0p})- ( \overline{}_{0p}-\overline{b}_{0p}) \right)^{2}}{P-1} \right]}.$$ standard error composite estimate calculated : $$\sqrt{\dfrac{\sum_{c=1}^CSE^2_{\varepsilon_c}}{C^{2}}}.$$ standard error difference element (\(\)) composite composite calculated : $$\sqrt{\dfrac{\sum_{c=1}^CSE^2_{\varepsilon_c}}{C^{2}}+\left(\dfrac{(C-1)^2-1}{C^2}\right)SE^2_a}.$$ \(\varepsilon\) represents statistic interest, subindex \(0\) indicates estimate using total weights, \(r\) indicates replicate total \(R\),  \(p\) indicates plausible value total \(P\),  \(c\) indicates element composite estimate value total \(C\).
